workspace = mssparkutils.env.getWorkspaceName()
 
if workspace == 'pins-synw-odw-prod-uks':
    storage_account_name = 'pinsstmpescproduksqz8bvk'
elif workspace == 'pins-synw-odw-dev-uks':
    storage_account_name = 'pinsstmpescdevuksw49hqz'
else:
    raise ValueError(f'Unknown workspace: {workspace}')
 
csv_base_path = f'abfss://mpesc@{storage_account_name}.dfs.core.windows.net'
 
print(f'Workspace       : {workspace}')
print(f'Storage account : {storage_account_name}')
print(f'Base path       : {csv_base_path}')
 
# ── 2. Load source files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
case_path      = f'{csv_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEET/EXTRACT/Case.csv'
authority_path = f'{csv_base_path}/config/Authority.csv'
 
print(f'\nReading Case      : {case_path}')
print(f'Reading Authority : {authority_path}')
 
case_df      = spark.read.option('header', 'true').csv(case_path)
authority_df = spark.read.option('header', 'true').csv(authority_path)
 
print(f'Case rows      : {case_df.count()}')
print(f'Authority rows : {authority_df.count()}')

case_df = case_df.withColumnRenamed('authorityId', 'legacyAuthorityId')
 
# ── 4. Build the pinsCode → GUID lookup from Authority ────────────────────────
 
authority_lookup = (
    authority_df
    .select('pinsCode', 'id')        # id is the GUID we want to map to
    .withColumnRenamed('id', 'authorityId')  # rename so the join result is clean

from pyspark.sql.functions import col
 
enriched_df = case_df.join(
    authority_lookup,
    case_df['legacyAuthorityId'] == authority_lookup['pinsCode'],
    how='left'
).drop('pinsCode')

total_rows       = enriched_df.count()
unmapped_rows    = enriched_df.filter(col('authorityId').isNull()).count()
mapped_rows      = total_rows - unmapped_rows
 
print(f'\nTotal rows       : {total_rows}')
print(f'Mapped rows      : {mapped_rows}')
print(f'Unmapped rows    : {unmapped_rows}')
 
if unmapped_rows > 0:
    print('\nWARNING: Some cases could not be matched to an Authority GUID.')
    print('Unmatched legacyAuthorityId values:')
    (
        enriched_df
        .filter(col('authorityId').isNull())
        .select('legacyAuthorityId')
        .distinct()
        .show(50, truncate=False)
    )

# Re-read sources to clear the broken plan
case_df      = spark.read.option('header', 'true').csv(case_path)
authority_df = spark.read.option('header', 'true').csv(authority_path)

case_df = case_df.withColumnRenamed('authorityId', 'legacyAuthorityId')

authority_lookup = (
    authority_df
    .select('pinsCode', 'id')
    .withColumnRenamed('id', 'authorityId')
)

enriched_df = case_df.join(
    authority_lookup,
    case_df['legacyAuthorityId'] == authority_lookup['pinsCode'],
    how='left'
).drop('pinsCode')

enriched_df = enriched_df.drop('legacyAuthorityId')

# Then write
staging_path = f'{csv_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEET/EXTRACT/_Case_staging'
output_path  = f'{csv_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEET/EXTRACT/Case.csv'

try: mssparkutils.fs.rm(staging_path, recurse=True)
except: pass

enriched_df.coalesce(1).write.mode('overwrite').option('header', 'true').csv(staging_path)

part_file = [f.path for f in mssparkutils.fs.ls(staging_path) if f.name.startswith('part-') and f.name.endswith('.csv')][0]

try: mssparkutils.fs.rm(output_path, recurse=True)
except: pass

mssparkutils.fs.mv(part_file, output_path)
mssparkutils.fs.rm(staging_path, recurse=True)

print('Migration complete.')
